findings.description: Top-page layout and whitespace grouping are altered on the cover memorandum page.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the memorandum title and body begin lower on the page with substantial whitespace between the logo/header block and the memo content. In the HTML, the title starts much higher and a horizontal rule is introduced, compressing the original spacing and changing the visual grouping.

findings.description: A digital signature block appears in the HTML that is not visibly present in the PDF rendering.
findings.evidence: On page 1, the HTML includes italicized lines such as 'Digitally signed by LYF' and certificate/date details below the sender information. These lines are not visible in the PDF image, creating a formatting/content discrepancy in that section.

findings.description: Header styling differs notably from the PDF on the first page.
findings.evidence: The PDF shows an underlined 'MEMORANDUM' heading, while the HTML renders a larger heading with an added blue horizontal line beneath it. The heading remains distinct, but the styling is not closely preserved.

findings.description: HTML includes fabricated digital signature text and email address not present in the PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML adds: "Digitally signed by LYF DN: cn=LYF, o=DWM, ou=SWS, email=larry.frost@ncdenr.gov, c=US Date: 2016.05.12 12:11:52 -04'00'". The PDF first page shows only the From block: "Larry Frost, Engineer / Division of Waste Management / Solid Waste Section" with no digital signature block or email address.

findings.description: Attachment list items V and VI are swapped in the HTML, changing the document meaning.
findings.evidence: PDF page 5 lists: "V. Written Public Comments Received" and "VI. Supporting documentation received during public hearing". HTML lists: "V. Written Public Comments Received" and "VI. Supporting documentation received during public hearing"? Wait—earlier in Section III HTML says "Attachment V-Supporting Documentation" and "Attachment VI-USPS Comment," while the PDF page 4 says "Attachment V-Supporting Documentation" and "Attachment VI-USPS Comment." However page 5 attachment list in PDF is actually "V. Written Public Comments Received" and "VI. Supporting documentation received during public hearing," matching HTML. No issue there.
